profile.name: Talank Baral
profile.title: PhD Student in Computer Science
profile.bio: I am a PhD student in Computer Science at George Mason University specializing in software engineering. My research focuses on improving the speed and reliability of software testing and continuous integration using empirical methods and large language models (LLMs). I work on problems such as flaky test analysis and test execution optimization.

news.html: Paper "TDRepro: A Neurosymbolic Approach to Reproducing Timing-Dependent Flaky Test Failures" accepted to ASE 2026! 🎉
Paper "Test Coverage Analysis of Agentic Pull Requests" accepted to ICSME 2026! 🎉

publications.venue: In ICSE 2025, Proceedings of the 50th International Conference on Software Engineering, Demonstrations track, Ottawa, Ontario, Canada, 2025.

publications.title: Optimizing Continuous Development By Detecting and Preventing Unnecessary Content Generation
publications.venue: In ASE 2023, Proceedings of the 38th Annual International Conference on Automated Software Engineering (ASE 2023), pages 901-913, Kirchberg, Luxembourg, Sept. 2023.
